fixes_applied: tests/test.sh: restore from tests/test.sh.legacy.bak — the in-place test.sh is the SWE-rebench fallback stub (`FAIL_TO_PASS=[]`, `PASS_TO_PASS=[]`) that runs `bun x vitest run` and falls through to overall pass-rate, scoring nothing meaningful for this task. The legacy.bak is a purpose-built behavioral verifier with 5 well-designed gates (1 P2P_GATING + 4 F2P) covering both the changelog audit and the autocomplete fix.

tests/test_manifest.yaml: restore from tests/test_manifest.yaml.legacy.bak — F2P weights sum to 1.0 (t1_f2p_changelog_unreleased_grew=0.25, t1_f2p_changelog_attribution_format=0.25, t7_f2p_autocomplete_dir_no_trailing_space=0.30, t7_f2p_autocomplete_dir_marker=0.20). All 4 v043 P2P_REGRESSION upstream gates already declared with weight=0.0 (informational).
tests/install_config.json: leave as-is (will not be read once test.sh is restored to the legacy gate-based form).

validation_results.reasoning: Predicted from patch_library inspection (no docker run available). Nop baseline: no CHANGELOG growth (GROWN=0 < 2 → t1a FAIL); no new attributed bullets (NEW_BULLETS=0 → t1b FAIL); autocomplete probe sees no dir-marker logic and SOURCE_HAS_RUNTIME_DIR_LOGIC=0 in the buggy state → t7a/t7b FAIL. P2P changelog structure intact → not zeroed, but no F2P pass → reward=0.0. Canonical/opus46 patch (29 ops): adds [Unreleased] bullets to packages/{ai,tui,coding-agent}/CHANGELOG.md with `([#N](pull/N) by [@user](url))` attribution → t1a PASS (3 changelogs grew) + t1b PASS (≥2 attributed); edits packages/tui/src/components/editor.ts to check `selected.label.endsWith("/")` and call `forceFileAutocomplete(true)` for directory completions → directory marker is present and runtime dir-logic is in the source → t7a PASS + t7b PASS. Reward = 0.25+0.25+0.30+0.20 = 1.00.

rescue_reasoning: Prior DROP claim — 'meta task / editorial / not benchmarkable' — is wrong on three independent grounds. (1) The deliverable is concretely testable: the legacy verifier does NOT grade prose, it counts CHANGELOG bullet additions and verifies they include the canonical PR-link attribution regex `\(\[#N\]\(.../pi-mono/(issues|pull)/N\)( by \[@user\]\(...\))?\)`. This is an objective, line-anchored format check — not subjective editorial review. (2) The session is NOT pure changelog editing; turn 7 (467s gap, the largest in the session) pivots to a real code fix in packages/tui/src/components/editor.ts for the directory-autocomplete trailing-space bug from PR #882. The legacy verifier dedicates 50% of weight (t7a=0.30, t7b=0.20) to behaviorally invoking the autocomplete module via tsx and inspecting whether dir entries are distinguishable AND lack a trailing-space marker — this is hard, behavioral, and implementation-agnostic. (3) The patch_library is dispositive: opus46 produced 29 ops touching CHANGELOG.md across 4 packages PLUS editor.ts with the exact `endsWith("/")` + `forceFileAutocomplete(true)` pattern the verifier rewards; deepseek_v4_pro produced 49 ops including editor.ts + vitest.config.ts; minimax27 even authored autocomplete.test.ts. Multi-model agents are demonstrably executing this task and producing structurally-distinct artifacts the verifier can grade. The 'changelog audit is subjective' claim is also defused by the verifier's design: t1a only requires ≥2 of 3 changelogs to grow (not which entries), and t1b only requires ≥2 attributed bullets (not which PRs) — both checks are uniqueness-tolerant and do not pin to the gold session's specific PR list. The fix_summary.md confirms validated discrimination (Sonnet 4.6 = 1.00, Haiku 4.5 = 0.75, gap=0.25 driven by attribution gate) so the gates are known to actually separate models in practice.
smoking_gun: harbor_tasks/pi-mono-auto-ec7037ba/tests/test.sh.legacy.bak lines 39–432 implement four behavioral gates (changelog growth count, attribution-regex match, tsx-import autocomplete probe with dir-marker + no-trailing-space checks, AST runtime-dir-logic guard against decorative comment-only patches) — none of which are 'editorial'. The active test.sh is the auto-generated SWE-rebench stub with empty FAIL_TO_PASS — that is a tooling artifact, not evidence the task is unbenchmarkable.
notes: The current test.sh appears to have been auto-overwritten by data-pipeline/scaffold/build_swerebench_configs.py (note `_upgraded_from_legacy: true` flag in install_config.json) — this is the same regression pattern seen in other DROP→RESCUE pi-mono cases. Restore is purely mechanical: `cp tests/test.sh.legacy.bak tests/test.sh && cp tests/test_manifest.yaml.legacy.bak tests/test_manifest.yaml`. No image rebuild needed (test.sh is mounted at runtime). The legacy verifier already follows v0.4.3.1 conventions: P2P_REGRESSION gates declared with weight=0.0, p2p_failed never zeros reward, the trailing v043 Python block recomputes reward via WEIGHTS dict with hard-zero only on P2P_GATING failure. One minor concern: the t7 behavioral probe requires tsx/bun/npx in PATH inside the verifier sandbox — this is a repo-deps assumption that may need a workspace `npm install` to have run during the agent's session for `node_modules/.bin/tsx` to exist; if it doesn't, t7a/t7b silently FAIL via the HAS_RESULTS=0 fallback (conservative — penalizes agents who didn't install deps, but doesn't false-positive). Acceptable trade-off.
